name: Passage BIO, Inc.
business: Passage BIO is a clinical-stage genetic medicines company focused on developing one-time gene therapies for neurodegenerative diseases. Its lead product candidate, PBFT02, is a gene replacement therapy for frontotemporal dementia caused by progranulin deficiency (FTD-GRN). The company is also exploring PBFT02 for other neurodegenerative conditions including FTD-C9orf72, ALS, Alzheimer's disease, and has a preclinical program for Huntington's disease.
sellsTo: Patients with Alzheimer's disease (GRN SNP carriers)

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical stage genetic medicines company focused on improving the lives of patients with neurodegenerative diseases.

catalysts.event: Report updated interim safety and biomarker data from Dose 2 in FTD patients
catalysts.sentence: Report updated interim safety and biomarker data from Dose 2 in FTD patients in the first half of 2026;

catalysts.event: Seek regulatory feedback on registrational trial design in FTD-GRN
catalysts.sentence: Seek regulatory feedback on registrational trial design in FTD-GRN in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Declare clinical candidate for Huntington's disease program
catalysts.sentence: We expect to declare a clinical candidate for this program in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Enrolling patients in Cohort 3 of upliFT-D trial
catalysts.sentence: As of March 2026, we are enrolling patients in Cohort 3 across our global trial sites.

tamClaims.claim: Prevalence of FTD-GRN in US and Europe approximately 18,000
tamClaims.sentence: Based on available literature, we estimate the prevalence of FTD-GRN in the United States and Europe is approximately 18,000, based on available literature.

tamClaims.claim: Prevalence of FTD-C9orf72 in US and Europe approximately 21,000
tamClaims.sentence: Based on available literature, we estimate the prevalence of FTD-C9orf72 in the United States and Europe is approximately 21,000.

tamClaims.claim: Prevalence of Huntington's disease in US and Europe approximately 70,000
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the prevalence of HD in the United States and Europe is approximately 70,000, based on available literature.
